description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a polished wooden exterior and rounded edges, evoking mid-20th-century design. The front panel features a large, rectangular speaker grille covered in a light mesh, flanked by four black control knobs along the bottom edge for tuning and volume adjustment. Above the speaker grille, a circular tuning window is centrally positioned, and a horizontal strip divides the front visually. At the top, a transparent frequency indicator panel with printed station markings is supported by two black mounts, and a thin antenna rod extends upward behind it. The back panel is dark, perforated for ventilation, and includes several cutouts for connectivity or maintenance. The sides are smooth, with one side showing a circular port, likely for external connections or ventilation.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a transparent frequency indicator, black control knobs, and a perforated back panel. The design features rounded edges and a prominent speaker grille.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, four black control knobs, a central tuning window, and the transparent frequency indicator panel at the top.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the smooth wooden surface, the top-mounted frequency indicator, and the protruding control knobs at the front edge.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the dark, perforated back panel with multiple ventilation holes and cutouts, as well as the antenna and frequency indicator panel above.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the smooth wooden surface and a circular port near the back, with the frequency indicator and antenna visible on top.
